verse.arabic_text: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا ٱلذِّكْرَ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَحَٰفِظُونَ
verse.translation: Indeed, it is We who sent down the Qur'an and indeed, We will be its guardian.

theme_question.correct: The divine promise to protect the Quran from corruption.
options: The divine promise to protect the Quran from corruption.
The command for believers to memorize the entire Quran.
The challenge to produce a chapter like the Quran.
The description of the Quran as a cure for the heart.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse was revealed in Makkah as a direct response to the disbelievers who were mocking the Prophet (ﷺ) and casting doubt on the divine origin of the Quran. It is a powerful declaration from Allah Himself, affirming that He is both the source of the revelation and its ultimate protector against any alteration or loss.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is the miraculous and divine preservation of the Quran (Hifdh). Unlike previous scriptures, which were entrusted to people and subsequently altered, Allah takes personal responsibility for guarding the final revelation. This divine promise has been fulfilled for over 14 centuries through the dual methods of mass memorization (oral tradition) and meticulous written preservation, ensuring its purity for all time.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-Hijr is a Makkan surah that takes its name from the 'Rocky Tract' inhabited by the people of Thamud. The surah warns the Quraysh by recounting the stories of previous nations who rejected their prophets. It emphasizes the truth of the revelation, the signs of Allah in creation, the certainty of the Day of Judgment, and offers comfort and strength to the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) in the face of rejection.
